text_content: DITA topic is the base unit of content. Every topic has: title (required), optional shortdesc/prolog, body (required), optional related-links, nested topics. Topic requires @id attribute. Topic types: concept, task, reference.
DITA map organizes topics. Map contains topicref, mapref, keydef, topicmeta. Maps define navigation and key scope. Root map establishes keyscope for keydef and keyref resolution.

topicref references a topic or map. Can nest to create hierarchy. Attributes: href (path to .dita or .ditamap), type (topic|map), keyscope (for key resolution). topicref can contain nested topicref or mapref.

conref (content reference) reuses content from another element. Attribute conref points to target: file.dita#id or file.dita#id/element. Target must be same element type or specialize from it. conrefend pairs with conref for range.

keyref references content by key. Key is defined by keydef in map. keyref resolves to target topic or element. Use keyscope to disambiguate when multiple maps define same key. keyref can replace element content.
keydef defines a key in a map. Keys can be scoped with keyscope. keydef href points to target resource. Keys enable indirect references via keyref. keydef is a specialization of topicref.
keyscope creates a scope for key resolution. Child keydefs are in that scope. keyref with keyscope prefix (e.g. scope1/keyname) resolves within that scope. Prevents key collisions across submaps.
body contains main topic content. Can contain: sections, examples, div, bodydiv. Block elements: p, ul, ol, dl, fig, table, simpletable, codeblock. Phrase: ph, keyword, term, xref.

section divides body content. Has title (optional) and block content. Sections cannot nest inside sections. Use example for nested structure. section can contain div, sectiondiv, p, ul, ol, fig, example, note.
example contains sample content. Has optional title. Examples cannot contain sections. Use for code samples, procedures. example can contain p, codeblock, steps.
prolog contains metadata: author, copyright, metadata, resourceid. Not displayed in output. Used for filtering, search, conditional processing.
shortdesc is brief description, often used in search results and link previews. One per topic. Inline content only.
xref creates cross-reference link. href or keyref points to target. format=dita for DITA content. scope=external for external URLs.
fig contains figure with optional title and image or other content. Use image for graphics. fig can contain title, image, desc.
table is formal table with thead, tbody, row, entry. Use simpletable for simpler tables. table requires proper structure for accessibility.
codeblock contains preformatted code. outputclass for syntax highlighting. Use coderef for conref to external code file.
ditamap file extension .ditamap. Root element is map. Contains topicref, mapref, keydef. Defines document structure and key scope.
mapref references another map. Creates hierarchy. Child map keys are in scope. format=dita for DITA maps.
conrefend pairs with conref to reference a range of elements. conref points to start, conrefend to end. Both must resolve to same parent.
ph is generic phrase element for inline content. Use when no semantic element fits. Can conref to replace content.
keyword marks a key term. Used for indexing. Can nest in term for glossary-style definitions.
topicmeta holds metadata for topicref/mapref: navtitle, linktext, searchtitle. Overrides topic content for navigation.
navtitle overrides topic title in TOC. Use when topic title is long or technical. Child of topicmeta.
image references graphic. href to file. alt for accessibility. Placement: inline or block. format for image type.
steps is ordered procedure. Contains step elements. Each step has cmd (required) and optional info, stepresult.
step is single step in procedure. cmd is the instruction. info for additional info. stepresult for outcome.
concept is topic type for conceptual information. Specializes topic. Typically has multiple sections. Use for explanations.
task is topic type for procedures. Specializes topic. Contains context, steps, result. Use for how-to content.
reference is topic type for reference material. Specializes topic. Use for API docs, specs, lookup tables.
div groups block content. Use for logical grouping. Can contain sections, examples, p, ul, ol.
simpletable is simpler than formal table. Contains sthead, strow, stentry. Use for key-value or simple grids.
desc provides description for figures, tables. Required for accessibility. Inline or block content.
taskbody contains task content: context, steps, result. Specializes body. Required in task topic.
conbody contains concept content. Specializes body. Use for conceptual explanations.
refbody contains reference content. Specializes body. Use for API docs, property tables.

reltable stores linking relationships between topics in a map. Preferred over topic-level links for content reuse. Contains relrow elements. Common pattern: 3 columns for concept, task, reference topics. Map-based linking reduces topic dependencies.
relrow is a row in a reltable. Each relrow contains relcolspec elements. Topics in the same row are linked. Avoid nested topicref within relcells.
relcolspec is a column in a reltable row. Holds topicref elements. type: concept, task, or reference. linking: normal, sourceonly, or targetonly controls reciprocal link generation.

collection-type attribute on topicref defines sibling relationships. unordered (default): parent-child links. sequence: prev/next links for procedural content. family: sibling-to-sibling links. choice: one-of selection.

linking attribute on relcolspec controls reciprocal links. normal: bidirectional. sourceonly: links only from this column. targetonly: links only to this column. Use to fine-tune reltable link generation.

Topic hierarchy best practice: aim for ~7 child topics per parent (Miller's Law), max 4 generations. Use single root topic. Prefer stub topics with titles over empty headings. Supports cognitive load and findability.

Reltable best practice: map-based linking preferred over topic-level links. Avoid nested topicref within relcells. Use descriptive reltable titles for author reference. Common design: source column and target column, or three columns for concept-task-reference.

Map structure best practice: one top-level topic from which all others branch. Use collection-type=sequence for procedural content (prev/next links), unordered for conceptual. Logical navigation and breadcrumbs depend on hierarchy design.

note is a callout for attention. type attribute: tip, caution, warning, important, danger, notice, remember, restriction, fastpath, trouble, other. Use othertype for custom types. Generates labeled output (e.g. Tip:, Caution:).
sectiondiv groups block content within section. No title. Use for conref grouping of related blocks. Can nest. Cannot contain sections. Use section or example for titled content.
bodydiv groups block content within body. Can nest. Can contain sections. Cannot be inside section. Use for conref grouping. No title by design.
p is paragraph block. Contains inline content: ph, keyword, term, xref, image. Common in body, section, note, step.
ul is unordered list. Contains li elements. Use for bullet lists.
ol is ordered list. Contains li elements. Use for numbered lists.
li is list item. Can contain block or inline content. Child of ul or ol.
dl is definition list. Contains dlentry elements. Optional dlhead for column headers. Pairs terms with definitions.
dlentry pairs term with definition in a definition list. Contains dt and dd.
dt contains the term in a definition list entry. Child of dlentry.
dd contains the definition or description in a definition list entry. Child of dlentry.
dlhead provides optional column headers for definition list. Child of dl. Contains dthd and ddhd for term/definition column headers.
dthd is term column header in dlhead.
ddhd is definition column header in dlhead.
prereq contains prerequisites before starting a task. What user must know or do first. Child of taskbody.
context provides contextual intro for a task. Child of taskbody. Optional.
result describes expected outcome after completing task steps. Child of taskbody.
cmd is the step command. Required in step. The instruction to perform.
info provides additional info within a step. Optional. Child of step.
stepresult describes outcome of a single step. Child of step. Used to verify step completion.
subjectScheme defines controlled values and taxonomies. Contains subjectdef, schemeref. Enables classification without modifying DTDs. Used for audience, platform, product, otherprops.
subjectdef defines individual subject or controlled value in subject scheme. Optional keys for identification. Can nest for hierarchy.
schemeref references external subject scheme. Child of subjectScheme. Imports controlled values from another scheme.
ditaval is filtering profile for conditional processing. Specifies include/exclude rules by audience, platform, product, otherprops. Used at build time for filtering content.

chunk attribute on topicref controls output. split: each topic as separate doc. to-content: merge topics. select-topic, select-branch, select-document. by-topic, by-document. Implementation-dependent.
audience metadata element for conditional processing. type: user, programmer, administrator. job, experiencelevel. Used for filtering and flagging.
platform metadata for OS/hardware. Conditional processing attribute. Used for filtering content by platform.
otherprops attribute for custom conditional values. Used when product, platform, audience do not fit. Supports grouped values.
properties is property list in refbody. Contains property elements. Use for API docs, parameter tables.
related-links section in topic. Contains link, linklist. Auto-generated or manual. Appears after body.

Self-reference: a topic can reference itself via keyref or topicref. keydef in map points to the same topic. Used for testing outgoing links panel, forward reference display. AEM Guides right panel shows self-reference as outgoing link.

Same-file xref/conref: use href="#topicId/elementId" or href="samefile.dita#topicId/elementId" for xref to section, li, fig, table within same topic. Use conref="#topicId/elementId" for same-file content reuse. conrefend pairs with conref for element ranges. Source and target must be different elements; avoid self-loop.
